subject: [PATCH v5 4/7] fuse: {io-uring} Allow reduced number of ring queues

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert disagreed that adding buf pools relates to queues, citing concerns about complexity and performance; he suggested using a single queue per CPU for lowest latency and proposed using core occupation as a trigger for adding more buffers or queues
reviews.raw_body:
Except of libfuse not being ready to create queues on demand, the series
basically supports that in kernel. And I was also thinking to use CU to
update the mapping.

However, I'm lost about the relation of buf pools. I very much disagree
that adding buf pools has a relation to queues. Unless you want to make
it 2D, which gets complex to find the right queue. Reasons:

1) If I start a queue in libfuse with a 1MB buffer and later see that it
gets used, libfuse should add more buffers. However, I would not want it
to add queues, unless I see that that ring threads occupy all of the core.

2) Lowest latency is still achieved with one queue per cpu - especially
here it makes sense to have a very low buffer usage and to increase it
if needed.

3) At least one customer at DDN sets a very specific cpu configuration,
using more cores is strictly forbidden.


Thanks,
Bernd

2026-06-23.patch_summary: This patch proposes to reframe the series around dynamic queue addition instead of using the FUSE_URING_REDUCED_Q flag, which is a narrow subset of this feature. The goal is to align with future uapi changes and make the code more cohesive. The main missing piece is adding infrastructure to publish the mapping as an immutable RCU snapshot, allowing readers to never see a partially-built mapping.
